scene: A visiting Pythagorean challenges you to public debate on the nature of the soul.

choices.label: Accept and prepare all night
choices.resultText: Neither yields, both gain. The crowd argues about it for a week.

choices.label: Decline - debates are theater
choices.resultText: You write instead. Quieter, deeper, less applauded.

scene: A merchant's dim but wealthy son wants to study under you. The fee would fund a year of work.

choices.label: Take him and his fee
choices.resultText: He learns little. The fee teaches you plenty.

choices.label: Take instead a brilliant poor student
choices.resultText: He pays in questions worth more than silver.

scene: A scroll questioning the gods' existence circulates secretly. A copy reaches your hands.

choices.label: Study it privately
choices.resultText: Dangerous arguments, annoyingly well constructed.

choices.label: Burn it publicly
choices.resultText: The flame is brief; the gesture, widely reported.

scene: The stoa by the harbor is free at midday - prime ground for a public lecture.

choices.label: Lecture on virtue and the city
choices.resultText: Even the fishmongers pause. Briefly.

choices.label: Lecture on the heavens
choices.resultText: Three listeners, one of them brilliant. A fair trade.

scene: A rival school spreads word that your teachings corrupt the young.

choices.label: Rebut them in the agora
choices.resultText: You quote their own master against them. It draws blood.

choices.label: Let your students' conduct answer
choices.resultText: Slow, dignified, and ultimately devastating.

scene: A House offers patronage - steady silver, in exchange for conclusions they will find agreeable.

choices.label: Accept the leash with the meat
choices.resultText: Comfortable. The word sits in your mouth like a stone.

choices.label: Refuse - thought is not for hire
choices.resultText: Poorer, prouder, quoted approvingly by people who pay you nothing.

scene: Your treatise on the mixing of peoples nears completion. Its conclusion will place you politically, forever.

choices.label: Conclude that mixing strengthens the polis
choices.resultText: Copies travel up the Rhone and across to Sicily. So does your name.

choices.label: Conclude that Hellenism must stay pure
choices.resultText: House Timon orders ten copies. House Kleitos orders none.

scene: Your best student is caught in a scandal with a magistrate's wife. His enemies blame your teachings.

choices.label: Defend him publicly
choices.resultText: Loyalty noted by everyone, approved by some.

choices.label: Expel him from the school
choices.resultText: The school is safe. The look on his face stays with you.

scene: An invitation: entertain a symposium of the powerful with philosophy, between the flute girls and the wine.

choices.label: Go and dazzle them
choices.resultText: Philosophy as entertainment. It pays better than philosophy as philosophy.

choices.label: Decline the circus
choices.resultText: An evening with your scrolls instead. No regrets. Few regrets.

scene: A student asks: if the oracle is always right, can a man choose otherwise? The priests are listening.

choices.label: Engage the paradox honestly
choices.resultText: A brilliant hour. A priest takes notes, not the friendly kind.

choices.label: Deflect with reverence
choices.resultText: The gods' ways exceed our grammar, you say. The priest relaxes.
